scene.description: Engine 181 is positioned on the street for a working car fire. A single 1¾″ attack line is stretched from the engine to the vehicle.  All scenario labels are generated from this JSON using overlay coordinates.

problems.title: Engine 181 Car Fire Attack Line
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating at a car fire. The crew stretched 150′ of 1¾″ hose with a fog nozzle flowing 150 gpm at 50 psi. There is no elevation change and no appliance loss. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = 52 FL + 50 NP + 0 elevation + 0 appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: Use the 1¾″ hose friction loss for the 150′ stretch, then add 50 psi nozzle pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes are using the wrong C value for 1¾″ hose, forgetting to convert 150′ into 1.5 hundred-foot sections, using 100 psi nozzle pressure when the question says 50 psi, adding appliance loss when there is no appliance, or rounding friction loss too early.

problems.title: Engine 181 Car Fire Attack Line
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating at a car fire. The crew stretched 200′ of 1¾″ hose with a fog nozzle flowing 150 gpm at 50 psi. There is no elevation change and no appliance loss. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = 70 FL + 50 NP + 0 elevation + 0 appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: Only the hose length changed. The longer 1¾″ stretch increases friction loss from 52 psi to 70 psi.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes are using the wrong C value for 1¾″ hose, forgetting to convert 150′ into 1.5 hundred-foot sections, using 100 psi nozzle pressure when the question says 50 psi, adding appliance loss when there is no appliance, or rounding friction loss too early.

problems.title: Engine 181 Car Fire Attack Line
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating at a car fire. The crew stretched 150′ of 1¾″ hose with a fog nozzle flowing 185 gpm at 50 psi. There is no elevation change and no appliance loss. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = 80 FL + 50 NP + 0 elevation + 0 appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: Only the flow changed. The higher fog nozzle flow increases friction loss in the 1¾″ attack line.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes are using the wrong C value for 1¾″ hose, forgetting to convert 150′ into 1.5 hundred-foot sections, using 100 psi nozzle pressure when the question says 50 psi, adding appliance loss when there is no appliance, or rounding friction loss too early.

problems.title: Engine 181 Car Fire Attack Line
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating at a car fire. The crew stretched 150′ of 1¾″ hose with a fog nozzle flowing 150 gpm at 100 psi. There is no elevation change and no appliance loss. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = 52 FL + 100 NP + 0 elevation + 0 appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: Only the nozzle pressure changed. The hose friction loss stays 52 psi, but the nozzle pressure increases to 100 psi.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes are using the wrong C value for 1¾″ hose, forgetting to convert 150′ into 1.5 hundred-foot sections, using 100 psi nozzle pressure when the question says 50 psi, adding appliance loss when there is no appliance, or rounding friction loss too early.
